svg.text: Page Caching — Fragmentation & Cache Key Explosion
Full page vs fragment vs ESI caching, personalization impact, and memory usage comparison
Three Levels of Page Caching Granularity
One cached page PER user PER page
Cache key explosion with N users
1. Template has ESI tags for dynamic blocks
3. CDN resolves ESI tags from origin
static at edge, dynamic at origin
Cache Key Explosion — Personalized Page Caching at Scale
Scenario: E-commerce product page with 10,000 products and 5,000,000 users
Each user sees: personalized recommendations, cart count, greeting, localized prices, A/B test variant
frag:header (1 key), frag:product:P001 (10,000 keys), frag:footer (1 key)
esi:page:product:P001 -- ONE key per product (10,000 keys)
Memory Usage Comparison — Full Page vs Fragment vs ESI
With 1% unique user-product combos: 500 GB still exceeds any single cache
Fits in a Redis cluster or distributed cache
ESI: ~50KB x 10K edge keys = ~0.5 GB (trivial at edge)
Mix of shared and per-user content.
Static at edge, dynamic at origin.
Key: Fragment or ESI for any personalization
